title: Improve activation for a new personal finance app
prompt: A personal finance app has strong signups but only 28% of new users connect a bank account in the first week. What would you do?

title: Design a product for new parents returning to work
prompt: Design a product that helps parents returning to work after parental leave. Who would you build for first, what jobs-to-be-done would you target, and how would you know it worked?

title: Design a product to prepare for a citizenship test
prompt: Design an end-to-end experience that helps an immigrant prepare for and pass a national citizenship test. Where is the biggest unmet need, and what would you ship first?

title: Improve content discovery for a streaming service
prompt: A major streaming service finds that 30% of sessions end without a play because users cannot decide what to watch. What would you investigate and change?

title: Build trust signals for first-time marketplace buyers
prompt: On a peer-to-peer marketplace, first-time buyers convert 4x lower than repeat buyers. How would you raise first-time buyer trust without making the product feel heavy?

title: Diagnose day-1 churn in a meditation app
prompt: A meditation app sees 60% of new users never return after the first session. What hypotheses would you test, and which would you validate first?

title: Order frequency drops in week 4 for grocery delivery
prompt: A grocery delivery app sees order frequency fall by 40% between weeks 1 and 4 for new cohorts. Walk through how you would diagnose and respond.

title: Design a product for small landlords screening tenants
prompt: Design a product that helps independent landlords (1-5 units) screen rental applicants fairly and quickly. What is the riskiest assumption, and how would you de-risk it?

title: Onboard switchers from a competitor
prompt: A productivity app sees a wave of users coming from a competitor that just had a price hike. How would you redesign onboarding for this segment?

title: Should a fitness app remove its free tier?
prompt: A fitness app considers removing its free tier to push paid conversion. What signals would you look at, and how would you make the call?

title: Telehealth scheduling drop-off
prompt: A telehealth product sees 40% of patients drop off at the scheduling step. How would you investigate, and what is the first thing you would test?

title: Design a college-application companion for high schoolers
prompt: Design a product that helps US high-school juniors and seniors plan their college applications. Where are the biggest gaps in current tools, and what would you ship first?

title: Reduce no-shows for at-home services
prompt: An at-home services marketplace sees 12% of bookings result in no-shows by the customer. How would you reduce that without hurting booking volume?

title: Choose metrics for a creator marketplace
prompt: You own a creator marketplace where brands hire creators for short campaigns. Define the North Star metric, guardrail metrics, and the first dashboard you would build.

title: Diagnose a 12-point activation drop
prompt: Activation just fell from 64% to 52% week-over-week with no known launch. Walk through your diagnostic playbook in priority order.

title: North Star for a B2B collaboration tool
prompt: Pick a North Star metric for a B2B collaboration tool that wants to deepen team usage, not just seat count. Justify it and name the input metrics.

title: North Star for a music free tier
prompt: Define a North Star and counter-metrics for the free tier of a music-streaming product whose explicit goal is to convert free users to paid.

title: A/B test a checkout redesign
prompt: Design an A/B test for a major checkout redesign. What are your primary, secondary, and guardrail metrics, and how would you decide significance?

title: Conversion up, session length down
prompt: An experiment shows +3% checkout conversion but -1% session length. Would you ship it, and what would you check before deciding?

title: Onboarding completion vs. day-7 retention
prompt: An onboarding change raises completion by 15% but drops day-7 retention by 4%. How do you frame the trade-off and decide?

title: Two segments, opposite signals
prompt: An experiment looks neutral overall but power users react positively while new users react negatively. What would you do next?

title: Engagement drops a week after launch
prompt: Engagement drops 6% the week after a feature launches, but the launch was small. How would you separate signal from noise and decide on rollback?

title: Retention up, ARPU down
prompt: Retention is up 5% over a quarter but average revenue per user is down 7% in the same window. How do you reason about it and what do you do?

title: Input metrics for a creator's first 14 days
prompt: You want creators on a UGC platform to reach 'activated' state in 14 days. Choose the input metrics you would track and why.

title: Power and runtime for an A/B test
prompt: Your team wants to detect a 1.5% lift in checkout conversion. Walk through how you would estimate the required sample size and runtime.

title: Drop-off at 'add payment'
prompt: On a subscription product, 38% of users abandon at the 'add payment' step. How would you investigate, and what would you ship first to lift conversion?

title: Design evals for an AI support assistant
prompt: Your company wants to launch an AI support assistant that can answer billing questions. How would you decide whether it is safe and useful enough to launch?

title: Eval framework for an AI code-review assistant
prompt: Design the eval suite for an AI code-review assistant that suggests inline fixes on pull requests. What dimensions would you score, and what is your launch bar?

title: When is human-in-the-loop required?
prompt: An AI scheduling agent can propose, modify, and confirm meetings on a user's behalf. Where would you require human-in-the-loop, and how would you decide?

title: Recovering from a viral bad answer
prompt: Your AI product gives a bad answer that goes viral. How do you recover trust with users, regulators, and internal stakeholders, in that order?

title: Latency vs. cost vs. quality on AI chat
prompt: You own an AI chat product. Walk through how you would balance latency, cost, and quality across model choices and routing. What does your dashboard look like?

title: Fallback when the model is uncertain
prompt: Design the fallback behaviour for an AI feature when the model is uncertain. What is the user experience, what data do you collect, and what does success look like?

title: Should a startup fine-tune on customer data?
prompt: A startup is debating whether to fine-tune a model on its enterprise customers' data. What would you align on with legal, security, and engineering before deciding?

title: A new jailbreak goes viral
prompt: A new jailbreak technique against your AI assistant goes viral. What are the first three actions you take in the next 24 hours, and what is your 30-day plan?

title: Launch criteria for an AI sales assistant
prompt: Define the launch criteria for an AI sales assistant in a regulated industry (e.g., financial services). What is on the bar, and what would block a launch?

title: Evals for an AI K-12 math tutor
prompt: Design evals for an AI tutor for K-12 math. How do you measure correctness, pedagogy quality, and safety? What is the bar for general availability?

title: Third-party model vs. self-host
prompt: Your team is choosing between a third-party frontier model and self-hosting an open-weights model. Frame the decision and recommend an approach.

title: Tell me about a disagreement with engineering
prompt: Tell me about a time you disagreed with engineering or design on what to build. What happened and what did you learn?

title: Should a B2B SaaS product add an AI copilot?
prompt: A mature B2B SaaS product wants to add an AI copilot because competitors are doing it. How would you decide whether to invest?

title: Build, buy, or partner: AI photo editor
prompt: Your consumer app wants an AI-powered photo editor. Should you build, buy, or partner? Frame the decision and pick a path.

title: Pricing for a freemium developer tool
prompt: Recommend a pricing structure for a freemium developer tool with strong individual usage but weak team monetization. What would you change first?

title: Reposition against an upmarket competitor
prompt: A low-end CRM faces a stronger upmarket competitor moving down. How would you reposition the product, and what would you give up?

title: Bundle or charge separately for AI?
prompt: Should a productivity SaaS bundle a new AI feature into the existing plans or sell it as a paid add-on? Make the call and defend it.

title: Move down to SMB or stay enterprise?
prompt: An enterprise SaaS sees growth slowing. The CEO asks if you should open an SMB tier. How do you analyze and recommend?

title: Pivot from feature to platform
prompt: A successful single-feature product wants to become a platform. When is the right time to pivot, and what would you ship in the first 90 days?

title: Cold-start strategy for a marketplace
prompt: You are seeding a two-sided marketplace from zero in a single city. Walk through your supply-side and demand-side strategy for the first 6 months.

title: Moat for an AI-native note-taking app
prompt: What is the durable advantage of an AI-native note-taking app three years out? What would you invest in now to make that real?

title: Plan a partner API launch
prompt: Your product needs to expose a partner API for enterprise customers. What would you align on with engineering before launch?
